schema_notes: Reference implementation for per-billionaire record. Every dollar has source_url + retrieved_at. Events are canonically scoped by event_role (pledge / payment / grant_out / transfer_in / direct_gift / political / private_investment). Classification for Tier A/B/C happens downstream, not in this file.
generated_by: scrooge v3 manual reference run — single-subject deep research (Alice Walton)

person.wealth_source: Walmart Inc. — only daughter of founder Sam Walton. Walton family collectively owns ~45% of Walmart through Walton Enterprises LLC and Walton Family Holdings Trust. Wealth held primarily via trust structures rather than direct equity. Also founder/principal of Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art, Art Bridges Foundation, Heartland Whole Health Institute, and Alice L. Walton School of Medicine.

net_worth.range_note: Forbes 2026 World's Billionaires list reports $134B — world's richest woman for third consecutive year, #14 globally. Bloomberg Billionaires Index July 2025 pegged her at $116B (#15). TIME100 Philanthropy 2025 cited $101B. Forbes 2024 cited $89.2B as the first female centibillionaire in America. All 2025-2026 figures broadly consistent with Walmart's 2025-2026 stock appreciation. Apr-2026 Forbes $134B is the primary estimate used here.

sources.publisher: Forbes (via CultureMap Fort Worth, reporting 2026 World's Billionaires list)

sources.value_note: Corroborates world's richest woman status and Walton Family Holdings Trust structure

net_worth.liquidity_notes: Wealth concentrated in Walmart stock held through Walton Enterprises LLC and Walton Family Holdings Trust — highly illiquid in practice because coordinated family divestment would move the stock and disrupt ~45% family control. She is not an operational executive, so she has somewhat more personal latitude than Rob (who was long-time chairman), but the wealth structure is effectively the same. Apply 20% liquidity discount (matching Rob's) for 'giving capacity now' calculations.

cited_events.recipient: Various (education, arts, health, economic opportunity — Arkansas, New York, Texas focus)
cited_events.notes: 100% attributable to Alice Walton (her personal foundation). She serves as Director/President with zero officer compensation. Managing Director Reuben McDaniel compensated $660K.
Foundation assets grew from $329M end-of-2019 to $4.69B by 2024 — implies large transfer_in from Alice's personal wealth ~2020-2021 (foundation assets leapt to $3.18B in 2020).

cited_events.recipient: Museums nationwide (Access for All grant program — 64 museums as of 2025)
cited_events.notes: 100% attributable to Alice Walton (her personal museum-lending foundation, founded 2016-2017). Alice Walton serves as Director/President with zero officer compensation.

Unusually large year — likely includes the $40M Access for All program launch (Oct 2023, 64 museums) plus major art acquisitions/loans. Art acquisitions to Art Bridges' own collection are not 'grants out' to third parties in the conventional sense; this number may reflect program service expenses including asset acquisition for the lending collection. VERIFY: separate grants-out to external museums from internal collection-building spending via 990-PF Part XV.
Spike year — likely includes large seed acquisitions for the lending collection. Same caveat as 2023 about art-acquisition vs. external-grant allocation.

cited_events.prorata_method: Per Rob Walton v3 record: divide WFF grants_out by 3 living siblings (Rob, Jim, Alice). Late brother John Walton's estate credited separately. Assumes roughly equal contribution share — actual split not public. Identical 33% method as Rob record for internal consistency.

cited_events.amount_usd_note: Approximation — Heartland Whole Health Institute's total assets grew from $5.1M (2019) to $598M (2024). Net asset growth primarily reflects transfers-in from Alice Walton or affiliated vehicles. Per dedup rule #2, we count HWHI's grants_out, not transfers IN.
cited_events.dedup_note: CRITICAL: This is a transfer INTO Heartland Whole Health Institute, NOT out-the-door giving to ultimate beneficiaries. Per dedup rule #2 we count HWHI's grants/program-services OUT. HWHI 2024 total expenses = $10.6M, of which ~$7.9M was charitable program services. Only $7.9M/year is observable out-the-door giving from HWHI, not the $598M asset base. Exclude the transfer_in from observable rollup.

cited_events.amount_usd_note: 74.4% of $10.56M total 2024 expenses reported as charitable disbursements.
cited_events.notes: HWHI is Alice's whole-health operating nonprofit (not a grantmaking foundation). Expenses are primarily program operations, not external grants. Counted as observable giving since HWHI is 100% Alice's vehicle but conceptually closer to Crystal Bridges (operating nonprofit) than to Alice L Walton Foundation (grantmaker).

cited_events.amount_note: Alice L. Walton Foundation side of a $700M total 30-year agreement. Mercy commits the other $350M. Alice's $350M funds outpatient center of excellence for specialty care + cardiac services + virtual care + physician recruitment in Bentonville.
cited_events.dedup_note: DEDUP FLAG: $350M pledge over 30 years. Will disburse through Alice L Walton Foundation 990-PF filings in future years as annual grants. DO NOT add this pledge row on top of future ALWF grants_out totals. Listed here as a pledge for provenance. Also note classification ambiguity: Mercy is a 501(c)(3) hospital system, so this IS charity; but it funds a joint-operated facility that advances ALWF's own Alice L. Walton School of Medicine ecosystem — arm's-length but related-party-adjacent.

cited_events.recipient: 64 museums across US (Access for All grant program)
cited_events.dedup_note: Flows through Art Bridges Foundation, so captured in artbridges_2023 or subsequent years' 990-PF grants_out totals as it disburses. DO NOT add on top of Art Bridges 990 totals. Listed here for provenance.

cited_events.recipient: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art
cited_events.amount_breakdown: $800M endowment (Walton Family Foundation) + $403M operating/construction/artwork + other capital gifts
cited_events.dedup_note: Pre-dates our 2019-2024 observable window. Flowed through WFF (captured in WFF pre-2019 990-PFs not in current window) + additional Alice personal contributions (untracked). Listed for provenance. Counting: do NOT add to observable rollup since it was pre-window and attribution between WFF and Alice personal is unclear.

pledges_and_announcements.quote: Per Walton Family Foundation communications, no members of the Walton family — including Alice — have signed the Giving Pledge. The position has been explicitly stated by Rob Walton ('We have chosen not to do that and to go our own way with our philanthropy') and extends to his siblings.
pledges_and_announcements.notes: Alice Walton has never signed the Giving Pledge — the position is a family consensus, not individual. Under scrooge methodology this is NOT a red flag — declining is more honest than signing-and-not-fulfilling — but it removes a public accountability hook.

pledges_and_announcements.status_note: Rob, Jim, and Alice Walton collectively pledged ~$2B to WFF between 2008-2013. Pledge was to the family foundation (a transfer_in), which has since disbursed well above that amount in grants_out. Pro-rata 1/3 share imputed to Alice.
pledges_and_announcements.dedup_note: Transfer INTO WFF, not grant_out. Per dedup rule #2 we count WFF's grants_out. This row is provenance-only; not added to observable_giving.

pledges_and_announcements.classification_note: Mercy is a 501(c)(3). Will flow through ALWF 990-PF grants_out over 30 years. See awf_mercy_700m_2024 in cited_events for dedup.

political_giving.substitution_note: Not counted as philanthropy. NBER W26616 estimates political giving displaces charitable giving at ~$0.33 per $1. Alice's political giving is lower-magnitude than Rob's ($17M 2023-2024 cycle) but has risen sharply in 2024 — historically she was more center/left-leaning than her brothers but has shifted rightward in recent cycles.
political_giving.observed_total_note: Reporting varies by source. United for Respect: Alice gave $5M in 2023-2024 cycle, primarily $5M to Americans for Prosperity Action (Koch-aligned conservative super PAC). Arkansas Times: 'more than $10 million' — likely includes state-level giving ($4.2M to state candidates/committees reported by FollowTheMoney). Combined federal + state figure approximately $9-10M. Historically Alice was among Walton family's more politically diversified donors (Democratic and Republican); 2024 shift to Americans for Prosperity is a meaningful move right. Lower than Rob's $17M but still a material escalation from historical norm.

sources.note: Primary federal cycle figure; $5M to Americans for Prosperity Action

sources.amount_note: Top 50 US political donor 2024 cycle; more than $10M combined (federal + state)

sources.note: Lists Alice at $5M; Jim at $10M. Identifies Americans for Prosperity as primary recipient.

sources.publisher: FollowTheMoney (National Institute on Money in Politics)
sources.note: State-level candidates and committees (Texas-attributed — her primary residence)

foundations_active.name: Alice L Walton Foundation
foundations_active.officers_compensation: Managing Director Reuben McDaniel $660K; Program Officer Ben Brantmeier $103K; Assoc. Program Officer Casey Maenner $75K. Alice Walton (Director/President) and August Martin (Treasurer/Secretary) both receive $0 comp.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants out ($51.9M) vs assets ($4.69B) = 1.1% — BELOW the 5% legal minimum for private foundations. 2023 was $100.6M/$4.05B = 2.5%, also below. Flag LOW_PAYOUT. Note: private foundation minimum-distribution calculation allows averaging across 5 years and certain exclusions; low-payout flag is preliminary pending 990-PF Part XIII detail.
focus_areas: Education (Alice L. Walton School of Medicine)
foundations_active.notes: Foundation assets leapt from $329M (2019) to $3.18B (2020) — large transfer_in from Alice personal wealth at establishment. Has been accumulating assets faster than it grants; 5-yr payout ratio trending low.

foundations_active.name: Art Bridges Inc (Art Bridges Foundation)
foundations_active.officers_compensation: Paul Provost (former CEO) $445K; Michael Shapiro (interim CEO) $167K. Alice Walton (Director/President) zero comp across all years.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: 2024 grants out ($35.2M) vs assets ($905M) = 3.9% — also below 5% minimum. 2023 was $389M/$868M = 45% (anomaly year, likely includes collection-building). 6-yr sum of grants_out = $663M vs avg assets ~$1.05B = ~10%/yr average. Not uniformly low but variable. Flag for verification.

foundations_active.name: Heartland Whole Health Institute
foundations_active.officers_compensation: Walter Harris (CEO) $770K; John Findley (CMO) $334K; Steve Macdonald (Treasurer, until 3/1/24) $212K. Alice Walton not listed as compensated officer.
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: Operating nonprofit — not subject to 5% payout minimum. 2024 total expenses $10.6M (of $598M assets) is low, but this is because HWHI is still in build-out phase ahead of the medical-school/Mercy expansion.
foundations_active.notes: Alice's whole-health operating vehicle. Parent org for Alice L. Walton School of Medicine (opened July 2025). Received large transfer_in from Alice ~2020-2021 (assets jumped from $5M end-2019 to $611M end-2021).

foundations_active.name: Alice L. Walton School of Medicine (AWSOM)
foundations_active.ein: not yet determined (likely operates under HWHI or as separate 501(c)(3))
foundations_active.notes: Opened July 14, 2025 with 48 inaugural students; tuition waived for first five cohorts. Separate 501(c)(3) from HWHI but shares founder/mission. VERIFY: pull EIN and 990 when available.

foundations_active.name: Crystal Bridges Museum of American Art
foundations_active.officers_compensation: Alice Walton listed as Board Director & Chair Emeritus, $0 compensation. (Executive Director/other officers compensated market rates — not fully extracted.)
foundations_active.payout_rate_notes: Not a grantmaking foundation — operating museum. 2024 expenses $96.1M. Free admission for all ~11M visitors since 2011 opening. Not counted as 'grants out' but is a material charitable service.
foundations_active.notes: Primary vehicle for Alice's cultural philanthropy. Received ~$1.34B cumulative from Walton Family Foundation through 2011 opening including $800M endowment + $403M construction/artwork. Alice's personal gifts above and beyond WFF flows are not separately disclosed. Free admission has cumulative economic value that is not captured in 990 grants_out metric.

detected_vehicles.dafs_note: No donor-advised fund activity identified from public sources. Absence-of-evidence only. ALWF and Art Bridges grant recipient lists should be parsed for DAF sponsors (Fidelity Charitable, Schwab Charitable, NPT, Vanguard Charitable) to verify no DAF-transfer pattern.
detected_vehicles.llcs_note: Walton Enterprises LLC is the family wealth-holding vehicle — NOT a philanthropic LLC. No Alice-specific philanthropic LLC identified (like CZI or Emerson Collective).
detected_vehicles.offshore_note: ICIJ Offshore Leaks name-match NOT YET RUN — deferred to Phase 2.

trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.source_quote: The majority of Alice Walton's fortune is derived from Walmart, with most of her shares owned through the Walton Family Holdings Trust and the family's closely held holding company, Walton Enterprises.
trusts_disclosed_existence_structure_unk.classification_note: Walton Family Holdings Trust is a wealth-holding / family-control vehicle. Specific structure (revocable, grantor, dynasty, CLT, GRAT) not publicly disclosed for Alice's portion. Like Rob, the family is known to have used estate-planning trusts historically. Dynasty-trust outcome is primarily wealth-transfer (zero charitable); CLT would guarantee future giving. Deferred Phase 2 SEC 13D/13G and estate-tax filing research.

red_flags.evidence: Alice Walton and all Walton siblings have not signed the Giving Pledge. Deliberate family position: 'We have chosen not to do that and to go our own way with our philanthropy' (Rob, applying to siblings).
red_flags.severity_note: Informational, not damning. Declining is more honest than signing-and-not-fulfilling. Under scrooge methodology we do NOT penalize declining the pledge — we only penalize breach.

red_flags.evidence: Alice L Walton Foundation 2024 payout 1.1% ($52M / $4.69B); 2023 payout 2.5% ($101M / $4.05B). Both years below the 5% legal minimum for private foundations. Private foundation minimum-distribution calculation allows averaging and exclusions so this is preliminary, but the pattern is concerning.

Alice's observable giving 2019-2024 from ALWF ($330.7M) + Art Bridges ($663.1M) + WFF 1/3 prorata ($1.195B) + HWHI 2024 program services ($7.9M, other years minimal) = ~$2.20B over 6 years vs. current net worth $134B = 1.64% of current NW cumulative, or 0.27%/yr. Forbes itself ranks her as having given ~1.64% of wealth lifetime — consistent with this bottom-up number.
red_flags.forbes_corroboration: Forbes 2025 philanthropy ranking places Alice in the second-lowest lifetime-giving category (1%-4.99% of wealth given).

red_flags.evidence: $5M+ federal 2023-2024 cycle giving (plus $4.2M state-level) — meaningful escalation from historical norm. $5M to Americans for Prosperity Action (Koch-aligned) represents a rightward shift from Alice's historically more diversified/center political pattern. Political giving substitutes for charitable giving at ~$0.33/$1 per NBER W26616.

Walton Family Foundation is funded collectively; individual contribution splits never disclosed. Pro-rata 1/3 allocation to Alice is a working assumption; actual share could differ. Creates ceiling uncertainty.

Art Bridges' $389M 2023 and $150M 2019 grants_out figures likely include art-acquisition spending for its own lending collection, not pure third-party grants. Building a collection that is lent for free is a legitimate charitable activity but is economically different from grants-out. Conservative treatment: count as observable but flag the ambiguity.

red_flags_not_present: HIGH_OFFICER_COMP (Alice zero comp at all her vehicles; staff comp reasonable for foundation scale)
DAF_TRANSFERS (not confirmed absent — recipient lists not yet parsed for DAF sponsors)
GIVING_PLEDGE_UNFULFILLED (never signed, so no breach is possible)

rollup.observable_giving_method: sum(ALWF grants_out 2019-2024: $330.66M, 100% Alice) + sum(Art Bridges grants_out 2019-2024: $663.08M, 100% Alice) + sum(WFF grants_out 2019-2024: $3.584B) × (1/3 Alice prorata: $1.195B) + HWHI 2024 program services (~$7.9M, 100% Alice; prior years immaterial). Rounded total ≈ $2.197B. Excludes: transfers INTO ALWF/Art Bridges/HWHI; Mercy $350M pledge (will disburse through ALWF future years); political giving; Crystal Bridges 2011 $1.34B opening gift (pre-window; attribution WFF vs personal unclear); direct-gift rows captured inside foundation totals.

observable_giving_prorata_sensitivity.note: Actual Alice-vs-Rob-vs-Jim WFF contribution split not public. If Alice contributed more than 1/3 (she is arguably the most publicly philanthropy-focused sibling, so this is plausible), observable climbs to ~$2.79B at 50%. Floor at 25% = ~$1.90B. Middle-case 33% used for consistency with Rob record.

observable_giving_todo: Pull ALWF, Art Bridges, HWHI 990-PFs for years before 2019 — foundations established 2016-2019 so pre-window is minimal, but HWHI pre-2019 may be zero.
Separate Art Bridges' art-acquisition spending from external-grants spending via 990-PF Part XV / Schedule I — currently counted together.
Parse ALWF and Art Bridges grant recipient lists for DAF sponsors to verify absence of DAF_TRANSFERS flag.
SEC EDGAR Form 4 for Alice Walton — check G-transactions (stock gifts) to non-foundation recipients not captured here.
Identify AWSOM 501(c)(3) EIN; check whether $700M Mercy agreement flows through ALWF, HWHI, or AWSOM directly.

Verify whether Crystal Bridges 2019-2024 operating contributions received include meaningful Alice-personal gifts vs. just WFF flows — if Alice has made personal Crystal Bridges gifts since 2019, add as direct_gift events.
Refine Alice-share prorata for WFF (arguments for >33%: she is the most publicly philanthropic sibling; arguments for <33%: she spent more on her own vehicles rather than WFF).
rollup.expected_giving_method: 10% × liquid_wealth × min(years_as_bil/10, 1) where liquid_wealth = 0.20 × $134B = $26.8B; tenure cap saturated (~30 years as billionaire). Expected = $2.68B. Strict denominator matching the formal 10% norm.

hidden_upper_usd.daf_uplift_note: No DAF evidence. Zero until recipient-list parsing confirms or denies.
hidden_upper_usd.offshore_uplift_note: Not yet checked (Phase 2). Zero until checked.
hidden_upper_usd.anonymous_cohort_method: BofA/Lilly 2024: 69% of older HNW donors give anonymously at least sometimes. Alice is relatively publicly visible (TIME100 Philanthropy 2025, extensive press for each initiative), which argues for LESS anonymous giving than median cohort. Apply conservative cohort-median: 0.2% of liquid wealth over 6-yr period = 0.002 × $26.8B × 6yr / 10yr scaling ≈ $322M; rounded down to $250M envelope given her publicity pattern.
hidden_upper_usd.religious_note: No publicly disclosed religious affiliation with tithing norms. Zero.
hidden_upper_usd.trust_outcome_method: Walton family trusts are primarily wealth-transfer / estate-planning vehicles (dynasty-style). No public disclosure of a charitable remainder structure for Alice specifically. Unlike Ellison, no explicit pledge-letter trust disclosure. Set to $0 — no basis to credit undisbursed trust assets toward charity.
hidden_upper_usd.joint_family_foundation_upside_method: If Alice's true contribution share of WFF is 50% (not 33%), observable rises to ~$2.79B — a $597M uplift vs. middle case. Plausible given she is the most publicly philanthropic of the three living siblings. Included as upside envelope; observable ratio published uses 33% assumption.
hidden_upper_usd.collection_building_hidden_method: Alice's personal art-acquisition spending for Crystal Bridges and Art Bridges collections has historically been very large (she purchased Thomas Eakins' 'The Gross Clinic' for $68M in 2006, Asher B. Durand's 'Kindred Spirits' for ~$35M, Norman Rockwell's 'Rosie the Riveter' for ~$5M, etc.). Some of this spending is captured inside foundation totals; some was pre-foundation personal purchases that were later transferred into Crystal Bridges/Art Bridges. Upper envelope on the hidden / timing-displaced portion: $300M.
hidden_upper_usd.total_method: anonymous ($250M) + joint-family upside ($597M) + collection-building timing displacement ($300M). DAF/LLC/offshore/religious/trust all zero.

rollup.tier_rule: VERIFIED_LOW requires (observable + hidden_upper) / expected < 0.2. Alice Walton's observable alone clears 82% of expected giving — ALWF + Art Bridges + WFF prorata + HWHI flows are real and substantial. With hidden upper she exceeds 100% of the 10% formal norm. She is NOT a Tier A Verified Low case. However, at $134B net worth she is still well below the ~10% lifetime threshold that would put her at Tier C (per Forbes herself: ~1.64% of wealth given lifetime). Giving is meaningful but lags capacity — especially concerning is the ALWF 1-2.5% payout rate, well below 5% legal minimum. Classification: Probably Low (Tier B) — gives visibly and at scale, but still far below the 10% norm and with the foundation-warehousing pattern Ellison also exhibits (large foundation assets not being deployed at minimum-required rates).
rollup.tier_published_caveat: Readers should see Alice Walton as 'the most publicly-visible Walton philanthropist — substantial and recognizable cultural + medical + museum-access giving, with zero officer compensation at her personal vehicles — BUT the foundations are accumulating assets faster than they deploy them (1.1% payout at ALWF 2024 is the signature red flag), and the $134B net worth is compounding faster than the giving. Forbes' own methodology places her in the 1%-4.99% lifetime-giving band. Observable ~$2.2B over 6 years is 0.27%/yr of current NW and 82% of the 10% formal norm. She has NOT signed the Giving Pledge (by family consensus) so cannot be in breach. Comparable reference class: other non-signers with similar wealth — where she looks relatively generous vs. Rob (more observable giving; less political giving) but still well short of the 10% formal norm.
rollup.expected_5pct_tenure_method: 5% × net_worth_best × liquidity_pct × years_as_billionaire (uncapped tenure); computed by aggregate_v3.py

scrooge_legacy_score_internal_only.note: Computed with v2 formula for continuity with current pipeline. No pledge breach (never signed). Tenure saturated (~30 years). Red flag penalty capped at 20pts from 4 active red flags × 5pts each. DO NOT PUBLISH this number — we publish tiers only per PLAN.md. Lower than Rob's 75.4 because Alice's observable giving is higher relative to her net worth (2.20B/134B vs Rob 1.52B/143B).

right_of_reply.planned_outreach_route: Alice L. Walton Foundation communications (alicelwaltonfoundation.org/contact) + Art Bridges Foundation press + Heartland Whole Health Institute press + Walton Family Foundation communications — four routes to maximize document delivery.
